name: Event Log Scanner
description: Scans Windows Event Logs for errors, warnings, and critical events within a configurable time window. Uses PowerShell to query multiple log sources and outputs a formatted summary.
author: sentinel-desktop

parameters.name: hours_back
parameters.label: Hours to Scan
parameters.description: Number of hours back from now to scan for events

parameters.name: log_name
parameters.label: Event Log Name
parameters.description: The Windows event log to scan (e.g., System, Application, Security). Use '*' for all logs.

parameters.name: min_level
parameters.label: Minimum Event Level
parameters.description: Minimum severity level to include: Error, Warning, or Informational

parameters.name: export_csv
parameters.label: Export to CSV
parameters.description: Whether to export the scan results to a CSV file on the desktop

steps.description: Open PowerShell for event log scanning

params.text: Write-Host '===== Sentinel Desktop — Event Log Scanner =====' -ForegroundColor Cyan
$startTime = (Get-Date).AddHours(-{{hours_back}}); Write-Host "[INFO] Scanning '{{log_name}}' log for events from $startTime to now" -ForegroundColor Yellow

steps.description: Calculate the time window and display scan parameters

params.text: $events = Get-WinEvent -FilterHashtable @{ LogName='{{log_name}}'; StartTime=$startTime; Level=1,2,3 } -MaxEvents 500 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object TimeCreated, Id, LevelDisplayName, ProviderName, Message | Sort-Object TimeCreated -Descending

steps.description: Query the event log for errors (1), warnings (2), and critical events (3)

params.text: Write-Host "[SUMMARY] Critical: $criticalCount | Errors: $errorCount | Warnings: $warnCount | Total scanned: $($events.Count)" -ForegroundColor $(if ($errorCount -gt 0) { 'Red' } else { 'Green' })

steps.description: Display a summary count of all event severities found

params.text: Write-Host "[DETAILS] Top 20 most recent events:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
$events | Select-Object -First 20 | Format-Table TimeCreated, Id, LevelDisplayName, ProviderName, @{L='Message';E={$_.Message.Substring(0, [Math]::Min($_.Message.Length, 80))}} -AutoSize -Wrap

steps.description: Display the 20 most recent events in a formatted table with truncated messages

params.text: Write-Host "[FREQUENCY] Top event sources by count:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
$events | Group-Object ProviderName | Sort-Object Count -Descending | Select-Object -First 10 | Format-Table Count, Name -AutoSize

steps.description: Group events by provider/source and show top 10 most frequent

params.text: $desktopPath = [Environment]::GetFolderPath('Desktop'); $csvPath = Join-Path $desktopPath "event_log_scan_{{log_name}}_$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss').csv"; $events | Export-Csv -Path $csvPath -NoTypeInformation -Encoding UTF8; Write-Host "[EXPORT] Results saved to: $csvPath" -ForegroundColor Green

steps.description: Export scan results to a timestamped CSV file on the desktop

params.text: Write-Host '===== Event Log Scan Complete =====' -ForegroundColor Cyan
